root_text.sn48.42:1.2: Atha kho uṇṇābho brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn48.42:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho uṇṇābho brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn48.42:2.1: “Pañcimāni, bho gotama, indriyāni nānāvisayāni nānāgocarāni, na aññamaññassa gocaravisayaṁ paccanubhonti.
root_text.sn48.42:2.4: Imesaṁ nu kho, bho gotama, pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ nānāvisayānaṁ nānāgocarānaṁ na aññamaññassa gocaravisayaṁ paccanubhontānaṁ kiṁ paṭisaraṇaṁ, ko ca nesaṁ gocaravisayaṁ paccanubhotī”ti?
root_text.sn48.42:3.1: “Pañcimāni, brāhmaṇa, indriyāni nānāvisayāni nānāgocarāni na aññamaññassa gocaravisayaṁ paccanubhonti.
root_text.sn48.42:3.4: Imesaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ nānāvisayānaṁ nānāgocarānaṁ na aññamaññassa gocaravisayaṁ paccanubhontānaṁ mano paṭisaraṇaṁ, manova nesaṁ gocaravisayaṁ paccanubhotī”ti.
root_text.sn48.42:4.8: “Accayāsi, brāhmaṇa, pañhaṁ, nāsakkhi pañhassa pariyantaṁ gahetuṁ.
root_text.sn48.42:4.9: Nibbānogadhañhi, brāhmaṇa, brahmacariyaṁ vussati nibbānaparāyaṇaṁ nibbānapariyosānan”ti.
root_text.sn48.42:5.1: Atha kho uṇṇābho brāhmaṇo bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.
root_text.sn48.42:6.1: Atha kho bhagavā acirapakkante uṇṇābhe brāhmaṇe bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn48.42:6.2: “seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, kūṭāgāre vā kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ vā pācīnavātapānā sūriye uggacchante vātapānena rasmi pavisitvā kvāssa patiṭṭhitā”ti?
root_text.sn48.42:6.4: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, uṇṇābhassa brāhmaṇassa tathāgate saddhā niviṭṭhā mūlajātā patiṭṭhitā daḷhā asaṁhāriyā samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.sn48.42:6.5: Imamhi ce, bhikkhave, samaye uṇṇābho brāhmaṇo kālaṁ kareyya, natthi taṁ saṁyojanaṁ yena saṁyojanena saṁyutto uṇṇābho brāhmaṇo puna imaṁ lokaṁ āgaccheyyā”ti.

translation_text.sn48.42:1.2: Then Uṇṇābha the brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn48.42:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn48.42:2.1: “Worthy Gotama, these five faculties have different domains and different ranges, and don’t experience each others’ domain and range.
translation_text.sn48.42:2.3: The faculties of the eye, ear, nose, tongue, and body.
translation_text.sn48.42:2.4: What do these five faculties, with their different domains and ranges, have recourse to? What experiences their domains and ranges?”
translation_text.sn48.42:3.1: “Brahmin, these five faculties have different domains and different ranges, and don’t experience each others’ domain and range.
translation_text.sn48.42:3.3: The faculties of the eye, ear, nose, tongue, and body.
translation_text.sn48.42:3.4: These five faculties, with their different domains and ranges, have recourse to the mind. And the mind experiences their domains and ranges.”
translation_text.sn48.42:4.1: “But worthy Gotama, what does the mind have recourse to?”
translation_text.sn48.42:4.3: “But what does mindfulness have recourse to?”
translation_text.sn48.42:4.5: “But what does freedom have recourse to?”
translation_text.sn48.42:4.7: “But what does extinguishment have recourse to?”
translation_text.sn48.42:4.8: “This question goes too far, brahmin! You weren’t able to grasp the limit of questioning.
translation_text.sn48.42:4.9: For extinguishment is the objective, destination, and culmination of the spiritual life.”
translation_text.sn48.42:5.1: And then the brahmin Uṇṇābha approved and agreed with what the Buddha said. He got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right, before leaving.
translation_text.sn48.42:6.1: Then, not long after he had left, the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn48.42:6.2: “Suppose there was a bungalow or a hall with a peaked roof, with windows on the eastern side. When the sun rises and a ray of light enters through a window, where would it land?”
translation_text.sn48.42:6.4: “In the same way, the brahmin Uṇṇābha’s faith in the Realized One is settled, rooted, and planted deep. It’s strong and can’t be shifted by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.
translation_text.sn48.42:6.5: If he were to pass away at this time, he would be bound by no fetter that might return him to this world.”

variant_text.sn48.42:4.8: Accayāsi → accasarā (bj, sya-all, km); ajjhaparaṁ (pts1ed, mr)
variant_text.sn48.42:6.2: bhikkhave, kūṭāgāre vā kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ vā → kūṭāgāraṁ vā kūṭāgārasālaṁ vā uttarāya (bj); kūṭāgāraṁ vā kūṭāgārasālā vā uttarāya (sya-all) | rasmi → rasmiyo (sya-all, mr) | kvāssa → kāya (sya-all, pts1ed, mr)
